fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: South Dakota Respiratory Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a respiratory therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to South Dakota - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: respiratory therapist license reciprocity south dakota, how to transfer respiratory therapist license to south dakota, respiratory therapist compact south dakota, south dakota respiratory therapist endorsement application

board.name: South Dakota Board of Medical and Osteopathic Examiners — RT
board.mailing_address: 101 N Main Ave., Suite 301, Sioux Falls, SD 57104

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) examination

license_types.title: Registered Respiratory Therapist (RRT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a respiratory therapist in South Dakota.

steps.title: Create Account & Start Application
steps.description: Visit the South Dakota Online Licensing Portal, create a new account, and then log in. Select 'Board of Medical & Osteopathic Examiners' and 'Respiratory Care Practitioner' to begin your initial application for licensure.

steps.title: Complete Online Application & Pay Fee
steps.description: Fill out the comprehensive online application form within the South Dakota Online Licensing Portal, providing all required personal, educational, and professional history. Pay the non-refundable $100 initial application fee during this step.

steps.title: Submit Official Transcripts
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts from your COARC-approved respiratory care program to be sent directly from your educational institution to the South Dakota Board of Medical and Osteopathic Examiners.

steps.title: Verify NBRC Credentials
steps.description: Request official verification of your Registered Respiratory Therapist (RRT) credential directly from the National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) to be sent to the South Dakota Board of Medical and Osteopathic Examiners.

steps.title: Complete Background Check
steps.description: Follow the instructions provided by the Board to complete a fingerprint-based background check through the designated vendor. Pay the associated state and federal background check fees (currently $57.50).

steps.title: Submit Verification of Other State Licenses (If Applicable)
steps.description: If you hold or have held a respiratory therapist license in any other state, jurisdiction, or country, arrange for official verification of licensure to be sent directly from the respective licensing board(s) to the South Dakota Board of Medical and Osteopathic Examiners.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBRC. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a South Dakota respiratory therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does South Dakota have reciprocity for respiratory therapists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers respiratory therapist practice in South Dakota. To work in South Dakota, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my respiratory therapist license to South Dakota?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in South Dakota while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Respiratory Therapist in South Dakota
hero.dek: Respiratory Therapists seeking initial or endorsement licensure in South Dakota must apply online through the South Dakota Online Licensing Portal.

variants.label: Renewal

official_links.registration_steps: To create an account, visit the South Dakota Online Licensing Portal, click 'Create New Account' and follow the prompts to register. Once registered, log in and select 'Board of Medical & Osteopathic Examiners' and 'Respiratory Care Practitioner' to start your application.
